from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# Predicate mapping for faster lookup
PREDICATE_MAP = {
    "country": lambda obj: f"from {obj}",
    "region": lambda obj: f"from the {obj} region",
    "mainIngredient": lambda obj: f"with {obj} as the main ingredient",
    "ingredient": lambda obj: f"containing {obj}",
    "dishVariation": lambda obj: f"with variations like {obj}",
    "course": lambda obj: f"served as a {obj}",
    "servingTemperature": lambda obj: f"typically served {obj}",
    "served": lambda obj: f"typically served {obj}",
    "servingSize": lambda obj: f"with a serving size of {obj}",
    "carbohydrate": lambda obj: f"containing {obj} carbohydrates",
    "fat": lambda obj: f"containing {obj} fat",
    "protein": lambda obj: f"containing {obj} protein",
    "division": lambda obj: f"of the {obj} division",
    "family": lambda obj: f"in the {obj} family",
    "order": lambda obj: f"under the {obj} order",
    "genus": lambda obj: f"of the {obj} genus",
    "class": lambda obj: f"classified as a {obj}",
    "alternativeName": lambda obj: f"also known as {obj}",
    "creator": lambda obj: f"created by {obj}",
    "creatorOfDish": lambda obj: f"created by {obj}",
    "year": lambda obj: f"introduced in {obj}",
    "related": lambda obj: f"related to {obj}",
    "similarDish": lambda obj: f"similar to {obj}",
    "foundationPlace": lambda obj: f"founded in {obj}",
    "leader": lambda obj: f"led by {obj}",
    "currency": lambda obj: f"using {obj} as currency",
    "demonym": lambda obj: f"with {obj}",
    "ethnicGroup": lambda obj: f"home to {obj}",
    "language": lambda obj: f"where {obj} is spoken",
    "officialLanguage": lambda obj: f"with {obj} as the official language",
    "leaderTitle": lambda obj: f"with the leader title {obj}",
    "capital": lambda obj: f"where the capital is {obj}",
    "isPartOf": lambda obj: f"part of {obj}",
}

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find main subject (most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))

    # Collect all information for main subject
    main_info = []
    related_info = {}

    for t in triples:
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_info.append((t.predicate, t.object))
        else:
            if t.subject not in related_info:
                related_info[t.subject] = []
            related_info[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Build sentence
    parts = [main_subject]

    # Add geographical context first
    geo_parts = []
    for pred, obj in main_info:
        if pred in ["country", "region"]:
            if pred in PREDICATE_MAP:
                geo_parts.append(PREDICATE_MAP[pred](obj))

    # Add related subject context (capital, isPartOf)
    for sub, triples_list in related_info.items():
        for pred, obj in triples_list:
            if pred in ["capital", "isPartOf"]:
                if pred in PREDICATE_MAP:
                    geo_parts.append(PREDICATE_MAP[pred](obj))

    if geo_parts:
        parts.append(", ".join(geo_parts))

    # Add other attributes
    other_parts = []
    for pred, obj in main_info:
        if pred not in ["country", "region"] and pred in PREDICATE_MAP:
            other_parts.append(PREDICATE_MAP[pred](obj))

    if other_parts:
        parts.append(", ".join(other_parts))

    # Join with commas and add period
    return ", ".join(parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)
Predicate: family - Example triple: (Almond | family | Rosaceae)
Predicate: order - Example triple: (Almond | order | Rosales)
Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")
Predicate: creatorOfDish - Example triple: (Asam pedas | creatorOfDish | Malay cuisine)
Predicate: creator - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | creator | "Rutland Arms, Bakewell, in 1820")
Predicate: servingTemperature - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | servingTemperature | "Warm (freshly baked) or cold")
Predicate: carbohydrate - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | carbohydrate | 18.0 g)
Predicate: fat - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | fat | 4.8 g)
Predicate: year - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | year | 1999)
Predicate: related - Example triple: (Bhajji | related | Pakora)
Predicate: similarDish - Example triple: (Bhajji | similarDish | "Pakora and other fritters made from wheat or corn flour")
Predicate: genus - Example triple: (Celery | genus | Apium)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (China | language | Standard Chinese)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Derbyshire Dales | isPartOf | Derbyshire)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (France | currency | Euro)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (India | demonym | Indian people)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Indonesia | capital | Jakarta)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (Java | ethnicGroup | Javanese people)
Predicate: foundationPlace - Example triple: (Mondelez International | foundationPlace | Chicago)
Predicate: officialLanguage - Example triple: (Philippines | officialLanguage | Philippine English)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (United States | leaderTitle | President of the United States)
Predicate: served - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | served | "Warm  or cold")
Predicate: protein - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | protein | 1.8 g)
Predicate: class - Example triple: (Chicken | class | Bird)
Predicate: servingSize - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | servingSize | 30.0 g)

from dataclasses import dataclass
from collections import deque

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find main subject (most triples)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))

    # Collect all information for main subject and related subjects
    all_info = []
    related_subjects = {}

    for t in triples:
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            all_info.append((t.predicate, t.object))
        else:
            if t.subject not in related_subjects:
                related_subjects[t.subject] = []
            related_subjects[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Build sentence parts
    sentence_parts = [main_subject]

    # Handle country/region relationships first (for geographical context)
    geo_parts = []
    for pred, obj in all_info:
        if pred == "country":
            geo_parts.append(f"from {obj}")
        elif pred == "region":
            geo_parts.append(f"from the {obj} region")

    # Handle capital/geographic relationships from related subjects
    for sub, triples_list in related_subjects.items():
        for pred, obj in triples_list:
            if pred == "capital":
                geo_parts.append(f"where the capital is {obj}")
            elif pred == "isPartOf":
                geo_parts.append(f"part of {obj}")

    # Handle other attributes
    other_parts = []
    for pred, obj in all_info:
        if pred in ["country", "region"]:
            continue
        if pred == "mainIngredient":
            other_parts.append(f"with {obj} as the main ingredient")
        elif pred == "ingredient":
            other_parts.append(f"containing {obj}")
        elif pred == "dishVariation":
            other_parts.append(f"with variations like {obj}")
        elif pred == "course":
            other_parts.append(f"served as a {obj}")
        elif pred in ["servingTemperature", "served"]:
            other_parts.append(f"typically served {obj}")
        elif pred == "servingSize":
            other_parts.append(f"with a serving size of {obj}")
        elif pred == "carbohydrate":
            other_parts.append(f"containing {obj} carbohydrates")
        elif pred == "fat":
            other_parts.append(f"containing {obj} fat")
        elif pred == "protein":
            other_parts.append(f"containing {obj} protein")
        elif pred == "division":
            other_parts.append(f"of the {obj} division")
        elif pred == "family":
            other_parts.append(f"in the {obj} family")
        elif pred == "order":
            other_parts.append(f"under the {obj} order")
        elif pred == "genus":
            other_parts.append(f"of the {obj} genus")
        elif pred == "class":
            other_parts.append(f"classified as a {obj}")
        elif pred == "alternativeName":
            other_parts.append(f"also known as {obj}")
        elif pred in ["creator", "creatorOfDish"]:
            other_parts.append(f"created by {obj}")
        elif pred == "year":
            other_parts.append(f"introduced in {obj}")
        elif pred == "related":
            other_parts.append(f"related to {obj}")
        elif pred == "similarDish":
            other_parts.append(f"similar to {obj}")
        elif pred == "foundationPlace":
            other_parts.append(f"founded in {obj}")
        elif pred == "leader":
            other_parts.append(f"led by {obj}")
        elif pred == "currency":
            other_parts.append(f"using {obj} as currency")
        elif pred == "demonym":
            other_parts.append(f"with {obj}")
        elif pred == "ethnicGroup":
            other_parts.append(f"home to {obj}")
        elif pred == "language":
            other_parts.append(f"where {obj} is spoken")
        elif pred == "officialLanguage":
            other_parts.append(f"with {obj} as the official language")
        elif pred == "leaderTitle":
            other_parts.append(f"with the leader title {obj}")

    # Build the final sentence
    if geo_parts:
        sentence_parts.append(", ".join(geo_parts))

    if other_parts:
        sentence_parts.append(", ".join(other_parts))

    # Join with commas and add period
    result = ", ".join(sentence_parts)
    return f"{result}."
